title: Personal Check-In Debug Survey
subtitle: A friendly playtest survey that covers every supported question type with easy personal prompts.
description: Use this survey for mass playtesting. It touches every question type while staying simple to answer: mood, gratitude, routines, priorities, and recent energy.
onboarding_subject: your recent emotional state and daily rhythm

audience_profiles.label: Participant
audience_profiles.description: Answering honestly about how the last little while has felt.

audience_profiles.label: Reflective
audience_profiles.description: Comfortable with longer answers and ranking tradeoffs.

audience_profiles.label: Quick Check-In
audience_profiles.description: Prefers a light, fast survey session.

guided_presets.label: Steady Week
guided_presets.description: For people who want to describe a calm or balanced recent stretch.

guided_presets.label: Stress Check
guided_presets.description: For people who want to reflect on stress, energy, and support.

guided_presets.label: Quick Pulse
guided_presets.description: For a faster pass that still touches the main features.

faq_items.question: Why are the prompts personal?
faq_items.answer: The questions are meant to be easy to answer quickly, while still exercising every UI path for playtesting.

faq_items.question: What is this template for?
faq_items.answer: It is a built-in debug survey for layout checks, answer storage, exports, scoring, and Journey flow playtests.

sections.title: Recent Snapshot
sections.description: Quick identity, timing, and context questions that are easy to answer without much setup.

questions.prompt: What name or nickname should this response use?
questions.description: Use whatever feels comfortable for this playtest run.

questions.prompt: What email should we use if we want to follow up later?
questions.description: Optional if you would rather keep this fully anonymous.

questions.prompt: What date are you answering this check-in?
Which age range fits you best right now?
About how many nights in the last week felt genuinely restful?

sections.title: Mood And Energy
sections.description: Questions about happiness, energy, support, and how the last little while has felt.

questions.prompt: How happy have you felt overall during the last week?
questions.description: Choose the score that feels most honest today.

questions.prompt: If someone asked how your week was going, how positive would your answer be from 0 to 10?
Have you felt emotionally supported lately?
questions.description: A simple yes or no gut check.

questions.prompt: Which of these have been bright spots for you recently?
questions.description: Choose as many as feel true.

questions.prompt: For each area below, choose what has felt closest to your recent experience.
questions.description: This matrix is meant to test partial and complete states cleanly.

sections.title: Values And Reflection
sections.description: A few easy reflection prompts about priorities, gratitude, and what matters most right now.

questions.prompt: Which area feels most important for you to protect right now?
Rank these activities by how important they feel to your well-being right now.
questions.description: Put the most important activity first.
questions.help_markdown:
## Ranking tip
Drag or move the options until the order feels right.

This prompt is here to test the full ranked-choice interaction using a subject that is easy to answer.

questions.prompt: What are one or two things you have felt grateful for recently?
questions.help_markdown:
## Gentle prompt
Write as much or as little as you want.

This question helps test long-form typing, wrapping, and saved answer recovery with something easy and personal.
